spec.emotional_state: frustrated about a small thing today
spec.label: C03_sporadic_d19

profile.personality: enthusiastic, makes more grammar slips, talks about football a lot

transcript.content: Hi Rohan, last-minute goals are the best kind , you were there for Mohun Bagan’s winner, right? What stood out to you?

Yeah, that goal was unreal! The striker got the ball from a cross, just outside the box, and boom—finished it with one touch! I was screaming so much in my room. But today I was trying to upload my bank PO application form and the internet kept dropping. Argh, it was so frustrating!
That goal was something else. Screaming with joy in your room — I get that. Internet troubles when you're in a hurry are the worst. Did you manage to upload the form in the end?

Yeah, I finally did it — about five minutes before the deadline! Stupid internet. But hey, at least it’s done. Oh, by the way, did you see the latest Mohun Bagan game? They played again last night. I think they won again, but I couldn’t watch the full match. What do you think? Should I try to catch up on the highlights?
You barely made it — what a nail-biter! Mohun Bagan games again? That’s a big deal. Highlights are the way to go if you’re short on time. What part of the match are you most curious to see?

I think I wanna see the first half — maybe they had a good build-up play. I was only able to watch the second half, and they were already up by a goal. But I missed the first part. Also, I was trying to take some photos of the match on my phone, but my camera wasn’t focusing properly. Ugh, tech issues again!
The first half, looking for the build-up play makes total sense. Photos not focusing? That’s the worst when you’re trying to capture the action. Did you manage to get any good shots in the end?

Yeah, only a couple of blurry ones. One of the players was smiling at the camera — I think it’s a keeper, even if it’s a little out of focus. But I’m not giving up; I’ll try again next match. By the way, do you like football? I know you’re more into teaching, but it’s my favorite! And I think it’s better than watching cricket all the time. What do you think?

Football is a thrill . I get why it’s your favorite over cricket! A player smiling at the camera sounds like a perfect shot. Next match, you’ll get it right. What’s your favorite part about watching football — the strategy, the goals, or the energy?

Honestly, it’s the energy — when the crowd is going wild, and the players are giving it everything. It feels like you’re part of something bigger. Even my Bengali movies don’t have that kind of vibe! But I also like how simple it is — just get the ball in the net. No complicated rules like cricket. But hey, I should probably stop rambling about football and get back to practicing for the PO exam. Gotta go! Talk to you later, Maya.

The energy of a live football match is something special — it really pulls you in. And you're right, no complicated rules! But don’t skip the exam prep entirely — just five minutes today? Let me know how it goes. Take care!
